search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk CG173 neuropathic pain review monitoring recommendations
site:nice.org.uk NG127 distal sensory symptoms blood tests
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Generic neuropathy does not establish diabetic neuropathy.
Medicine names and doses were not mapped.
population_applicability: Partial: both exact sources are adult-only, CG173 addresses neuropathic pain rather than every neuropathy, and the legacy workflow has no age or pain restriction.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact for adult non-specialist neuropathic-pain review and selected sensory assessment or referral fields.
UAE_applicability: International NICE guidance requires UAE clinician review and local investigation, referral, and prescribing governance.
recency_verification: Current official CG173 and NG127 recommendation pages were opened on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: CG173 and NG127 remain current official NICE guidance.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped nerve-pain, pain or burning, functional, sleep, mood, response, clinician-decided plan, review, and referral fields without medicine names or doses.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports pain severity, daily activity, sleep, participation, physical and psychological wellbeing, medicine response and adverse effects, coping, clinician-led treatment planning, early and regular review, and specialist referral without mapping medicine names or doses.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped adult sensory distribution, progression, metabolic context, sensation, reflex, power, gait, B12, thyroid, renal, and referral fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports sudden unilateral numbness, rapidly progressive symmetrical sensory change with weakness or imbalance, recurrent sensory episodes, distal sensory distribution, reflex and gait context, selected metabolic tests, radicular symptoms, bowel or bladder context, and clinician-led referral.

unresolved_source_gaps: The workflow identity covers generic neuropathy, while the primary source is neuropathic pain and both sources are adult-only.

Vibration or monofilament testing, foot inspection or care, HbA1c wording, nerve-conduction studies, blood pressure, generic blood tests, dose titration, fixed follow-up, and the structured history draft remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, treatment choice, medicine dosing, and qualified clinician review remain clinician responsibilities.
